channel.title: The Grant Desk - Research & Journalism, US, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Research & Journalism, US, next 30 days.

item.title: Justice + Joy Summer Research Fellowship 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Justice + Joy

Location: United States; fully virtual program. Capstone showcase tentatively in person (mid-September 2026), with travel and lodging covered.

Award: USD 2,000 stipend paid upon successful program completion, plus skills-based training, guest speaker sessions, and peer community. All travel and lodging for the in-person capstone are covered.

Duration: Seven-week virtual program, 9 July to 20 August 2026, with a capstone showcase mid-September 2026. Weekly Wednesday sessions 5:00-6:30 PM ET plus approximately 2-4 hours of independent work per week.

Deadline: 09 Jul 2026

The Justice + Joy Research Fellowship is a seven-week virtual summer program for girls, young women, and gender-expansive young people of color (ages 18-26) in the United States who want to explore issues impacting their lives, communities, and futures. Fellows identify a topic they care about, such as mental health, education, reproductive justice, immigration, AI and technology, gender-based violence, beauty standards, young parenthood, or another issue affecting their communities, and develop an original research project grounded in their experiences, questions, and ideas. Throughout the summer, fellows participate in weekly virtual sessions, engage with guest speakers, build community with peers from across the country, and receive mentorship and support as they develop their research. The fellowship culminates in a capstone showcase where fellows present their work to the Justice + Joy community, including organizational leaders, partners, and supporters. No prior research experience is required; the program seeks young people who are curious, committed to learning, and interested in using research as a tool for understanding, advocacy, and change. COMMITMENT: weekly virtual sessions on Wednesdays 5:00-6:30 PM ET (9 July - 20 August 2026); approximately 2-4 hours of independent work per week; required orientation on Wednesday 9 July; development of an individual research proposal or conceptual framework; presentation at the capstone showcase (tentatively in person, mid-September 2026, with all travel and lodging covered). WHAT YOU GET: a USD 2,000 stipend paid upon successful completion; skills-based training in research methods, social justice frameworks, and community advocacy; guest speaker sessions; a peer community; and a real audience for the research (the Justice + Joy board and funders). Apply via the Google Form. Note: an explicit application deadline is not stated; the required orientation and program start is 9 July 2026, so apply before then.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Civic Science Postdoctoral Fellow: Science Policy Collaborative (George Mason University) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: George Mason University (with UC Riverside), via the Science Policy Collaborative and Civic Science Fellows network

Location: Fairfax, Virginia, USA (hybrid eligible).

Award: Salary approximately USD 80,000 per year plus benefits. No application fee.

Duration: 18-month postdoctoral appointment (two contracts: 12 months + 6 months)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

A grant-funded, full-time Civic Science Postdoctoral Fellowship supporting the Science Policy Collaborative, a national network that strengthens the development, sustainability and evaluation of U.S. science policy programs. The fellow helps shape an emerging field at the heart of a growing community of practice connecting science policy programs across the country. WHAT YOU'LL DO: conduct a national needs assessment of U.S. science policy programs and publish the findings; support the Collaborative's working groups by scoping deliverables and building partnerships to execute them; develop infrastructure for knowledge exchange (tools like logic models and evaluation frameworks); connect programs with national and international experts on the use of research evidence (URE) and evidence-informed policymaking; present at partner meetings and convenings; and receive mentorship from George Mason University, UC Riverside and other leaders of the Collaborative. WHO THEY'RE LOOKING FOR: someone with a doctoral degree, relevant experience, knowledge of science-for-policy programs, and skills in network coordination, needs assessment or program evaluation, and writing for broad audiences (see the posting for exact criteria). Fairfax, VA (hybrid eligible); full-time 18-month postdoctoral appointment issued as two contracts (12 months plus 6 months); salary about USD 80,000/year plus benefits. APPLY with a cover letter, CV, three references and a writing sample written for broad audiences; for full consideration apply by 5 July 2026 (open until filled). Questions to search coordinator Natalie V Lapidot-Croitoru, nlapidot@gmu.edu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Future of Work Reporting Fellowship 2026 to 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Work Shift, in partnership with New America's Future of Work and Innovation Economy initiative

Location: United States (US-based journalists)

Award: $5,000 stipend + editorial coaching + access to expert sources + story amplification

Duration: 1 year (September 2026 to August 2027)

Deadline: 24 Jul 2026

One-year reporting fellowship supporting early- and mid-career US-based journalists to produce in-depth, place-based reporting on how education, workforce development and emerging technologies are reshaping economic opportunities across the United States. Open to print, digital, radio, television, multimedia and freelance journalists. Fellows receive a $5,000 stipend, editorial coaching, expert-source access and amplification of their stories.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
